Findings.Title: Block legacy authentication protocols
Findings.Detail: Legacy authentication blocked by 1 Conditional Access policy(ies).
Findings.Remediation: Create a Conditional Access policy: All users, All cloud apps, Client apps = Other clients, Grant = Block. Roll out in report-only first.

Findings.Title: Phishing-resistant MFA enforced for privileged roles
Findings.Detail: No Conditional Access policy enforces a phishing-resistant Authentication Strength on privileged roles.
Findings.RequiredValue: FIDO2 / WHfB / Cert-based for all admin roles
Findings.Remediation: Create a Conditional Access policy targeting all privileged role members requiring FIDO2 or certificate-based authentication via Authentication Strengths.

Findings.Title: MFA registered for all enabled user accounts
Findings.Detail: MFA registration critically low at 80%. Enforcing AAD-3.1 CA MFA policy at this registration level will lock out 1 users.
Findings.Remediation: Enable Registration Campaign (Entra ID > Authentication methods > Registration campaign). Use Temporary Access Pass (TAP) for bulk onboarding without helpdesk calls. Do not enforce AAD-3.1 until registration exceeds 95%.

Findings.Title: MFA enforced via Conditional Access (not per-user MFA or Security Defaults)
Findings.CurrentValue: Security Defaults: disabled — tenant managed via Conditional Access
Findings.RequiredValue: Security Defaults: disabled; MFA enforcement via Conditional Access

Findings.Title: Security Defaults disabled in favor of Conditional Access
Conditional Access: Legacy authentication blocked
Findings.RequiredValue: Enabled CA policy blocking client app types: other, exchangeActiveSync

Findings.Title: Conditional Access: MFA required for all users on all cloud apps
Findings.Detail: No enabled CA policy requires MFA for All users on All cloud apps. Compromised credentials provide immediate account access.
Findings.CurrentValue: No MFA CA policy for all users
Findings.RequiredValue: Enabled CA policy: All users (exclude break-glass), All cloud apps, Grant = Require MFA
Findings.Remediation: Create CA policy: All users (exclude break-glass named location), All cloud apps, Grant = Require MFA. Deploy report-only first. Do not enforce until AAD-2.1 registration exceeds 95%.

Findings.Title: Conditional Access: MFA required for Azure management
Findings.Detail: No enabled CA policy enforces MFA for Azure management (Azure portal, CLI, PowerShell). App ID: 797f4846-ba00-4fd7-ba43-dac1f8f63013.
Findings.CurrentValue: No MFA CA policy for Azure management
Findings.RequiredValue: Enabled CA policy targeting Microsoft Azure Management app with MFA grant
Findings.Remediation: Add Microsoft Azure Management to existing all-users MFA CA policy, or create a dedicated policy. Covers Azure portal, Azure CLI, and Azure PowerShell.

Findings.Title: No Conditional Access policies remaining in report-only mode
Findings.Detail: Report-only policies provide zero enforcement. They generate logs but do not block or require anything.
Findings.CurrentValue: 9 report-only policy/policies: [SharePoint admin center]Block access from apps on unmanaged devices - 2026/01/15; Require multifactor authentication for all users; Require MDM-enrolled and compliant device to access cloud apps for all users (Preview); Require phishing-resistant multifactor authentication for admins; Geo-Blocking; Require Device authentication joining devices; Insider Risk Policy - Block; Insider Risk - MFA; Insider Risk - Allow
Findings.RequiredValue: All security CA policies in enabled (enforced) state
Findings.Remediation: Review each. Enforce or document acceptance with rationale and target date. Report-only provides zero protection.

Findings.Title: Conditional Access: Sign-in risk policy configured (Entra ID P2)
Findings.CurrentValue: Policy: 'Sign in risk - Block' [Risk levels: high]
Findings.RequiredValue: Enabled CA policy targeting sign-in risk Medium/High with MFA or block response

Findings.Title: Conditional Access: User risk policy configured (Entra ID P2)
Findings.RequiredValue: Enabled CA policy targeting user risk High with block or password change response

Findings.Title: Authenticator app number matching enabled
Findings.CurrentValue: Number matching: enabled (Microsoft default or explicit). Additional context: default

Findings.Title: Global Administrator count between 2 and 5
Findings.RequiredValue: Between 2 and 5 permanent Global Administrator assignments

Findings.Title: No on-premises synced accounts hold Entra ID admin roles
Findings.CurrentValue: No on-premises synced accounts found with Entra ID role assignments
Findings.RequiredValue: Zero on-premises synced accounts in any Entra ID directory role

Findings.Title: Global Administrator accounts are dedicated (no assigned licenses)
Findings.Detail: Licensed GA accounts are daily-use accounts — exposed to phishing, malware, and browser-based attacks during normal work. A compromised daily-use account with GA is an immediate full-tenant compromise.
Findings.CurrentValue: GA accounts with assigned licenses (likely daily-use): mlevorson@nextlayersec.io
Findings.RequiredValue: All GA accounts unlicensed — admin tasks use dedicated cloud-only account, daily work uses separate licensed account
Findings.Remediation: Create dedicated cloud-only admin accounts: unlicensed, no mailbox, used only for admin tasks. Remove GA role from daily-use licensed accounts. Assign daily-use accounts appropriate scoped roles only.

Findings.Title: PIM used for Global Administrator role (JIT access model)
Findings.Detail: PIM is available but not consistently applied to the Global Administrator role. Permanent standing access means a compromised admin account immediately has full tenant privileges.
Findings.RequiredValue: All operational GA assignments via PIM eligible; zero or break-glass-only permanent assignments
Findings.Remediation: Migrate all operational GA assignments to PIM eligible. Activation settings: require MFA, justification required, max 8-hour duration. Consider approval workflow for production tenants.

Findings.Title: Break-glass Global Administrator account exists
Findings.Detail: Break-glass candidate(s) detected based on heuristic (unlicensed, cloud-only GA, sign-in > 30 days or never). Manual verification required — credential storage, CA exclusion, and sign-in alert cannot be confirmed automatically.
Findings.RequiredValue: Verified break-glass GA: cloud-only, unlicensed, credentials sealed offline, sign-in alert configured, tested quarterly
Findings.Remediation: Verify manually: (1) credentials printed and stored in sealed envelope in physical safe, (2) account excluded from MFA CA via named location, (3) sign-in alert configured in Entra ID or Azure Monitor, (4) tested quarterly from known IP.

Findings.Title: Anti-phishing policy with impersonation protection
Findings.Detail: Anti-phishing policy with impersonation protection exists but has no active rule applying it to recipients.
Findings.CurrentValue: Policy 'Strict Preset Security Policy1757690553683' configured but no enabled rule targets recipients
Findings.RequiredValue: Policy + active rule covering all accepted domains or all recipients
Findings.Remediation: Create an anti-phishing rule: New-AntiPhishRule -AntiPhishPolicy [name] -RecipientDomainIs [domain] -Priority 0. Or use the Security portal.

Findings.Title: Safe Attachments policy with Block action
Findings.Detail: Safe Attachments Block policy active and applied to recipients.
Findings.CurrentValue: Policy: 'Safe Attachments' | Action: Block | Enabled: True
Findings.RequiredValue: Enabled Safe Attachments policy with Action=Block and active rule

Findings.Title: Safe Links policy enabled tenant-wide
Findings.Detail: Safe Links enabled but AllowClickThrough=True — users can bypass Safe Links warnings and reach malicious URLs.
Findings.CurrentValue: Policy: 'Protect Safe Links' | ScanUrls: True | ClickThrough: ALLOWED (gap) | InternalSenders: enabled | Teams: enabled
Findings.RequiredValue: ScanUrls=True, AllowClickThrough=False, EnableForInternalSenders=True, applied to all recipients
Findings.Remediation: Set AllowClickThrough=$false on the Safe Links policy to prevent users bypassing URL block warnings.
